title: Oldenlandia prostrata
intro: Oldenlandia prostrata, also known as creeping oldenlandia or creeping mallow, is a small, prostrate herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia prostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions and decoctions, often used to treat fever and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia prostrata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, it is related to plants such as coffee and gardenias. Oldenlandia prostrata is a small, herbaceous plant that is native to various parts of the world. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
uses: Oldenlandia prostrata is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, for its various health benefits. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, Oldenlandia prostrata is said to have antioxidant properties, which can help in protecting the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Furthermore, it is used to treat fever and other respiratory issues.

side_effects: Oldenlandia prostrata is a plant that may cause allergic reactions or skin irritation in some individuals, especially when handled without proper care. Prolonged consumption of Oldenlandia prostrata can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and decrease their effectiveness. In rare cases, Oldenlandia prostrata can cause respiratory problems or exacerbate existing respiratory conditions. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Oldenlandia prostrata for medicinal purposes.
